rest_connector_workflow.mode_overview:
This mode guides you through creating JSON configuration files (connector descriptors) for REST APIs to be used with the CP4D REST Connector SDK. The configuration maps REST API endpoints to relational table schemas that can be queried using SQL.
IMPORTANT: The workflow begins with Phase 0 (Deployment Setup) which MUST be completed first. See 0_deployment_setup.xml for deployment option selection and project structure setup.
rest_connector_workflow.workflow_phases:
Deployment Setup 0_deployment_setup.xml
Select deployment option (codeless or developer) and set up project structure. This phase MUST be completed before proceeding to API discovery.
true
API Discovery & Analysis 1_workflow.xml (this file)
Gather API information and analyze endpoint structures.
Configuration Generation 1_workflow.xml (this file)
Generate JSON configuration files based on API analysis.
Presentation & Confirmation 1_workflow.xml (this file)
Present configuration and gather user feedback.
Multiple Connectors Check 6_deployment_execution.xml
Ask if user wants to create additional connectors. If yes, return to Phase 1. If no, proceed to Phase 5.
Can repeat Phases 1-3 for multiple connectors
Deployment Execution 6_deployment_execution.xml
Select and execute deployment option: Local Docker, Serverless, Self-Hosted, or OpenShift. Build and deploy the connector(s) to the chosen environment.
true
Cloud Pak for Data Registration 6_deployment_execution.xml
Register the deployed connector(s) with Cloud Pak for Data. Process differs based on deployment type (codeless vs developer).
true User can choose to skip registration and do it later
<<<<<<< HEAD:.bob/rules-connectors-forge-rest/1_workflow.xml <<<<<<< HEAD:.bob/rules-connectors-forge-rest/1_workflow.xml ======= >>>>>>> ca1e779 (validation after registry):.bob/rules-rest-api-schema-mapper/1_workflow.xml
Registration Validation & Testing 6_deployment_execution.xml
Validate connector registration by running discovery tests. Mode suggests test configuration based on connector information. Executes OS-specific test scripts to verify registration success.
true Only available after Phase 6 registration is completed
<<<<<<< HEAD:.bob/rules-connectors-forge-rest/1_workflow.xml ======= >>>>>>> 5b82670 (mode can do everything):.bob/rules-rest-api-schema-mapper/1_workflow.xml ======= >>>>>>> ca1e779 (validation after registry):.bob/rules-rest-api-schema-mapper/1_workflow.xml

phase_1_discovery.title: API Discovery & Analysis
phase_1_discovery.description: Before generating any JSON configuration file, you MUST gather required information. Never proceed without complete information.

item.name: API Base URL
item.description: The complete base URL including protocol and hostname
item.ask_if_missing: What is the base URL of the REST API?

item.name: Authentication Type
item.ask_if_missing: What authentication method does this API use?

item.name: Endpoints to Map
item.description: List of specific endpoints the user wants to map
item.important: Only map endpoints explicitly requested by the user
item.ask_if_missing: Which specific endpoints do you want to map to tables?

response_discovery_method.title: Determine How to Obtain API Response Information
response_discovery_method.description: After gathering basic API information, determine the method for obtaining JSON response structures. This is critical for understanding the data schema and creating accurate table mappings.

step_1_ask_discovery_method.question: How would you like me to obtain information about the JSON responses for each endpoint?

option.name: documentation
option.label: Use API Documentation
option.description: I will read and analyze the API documentation to understand response structures. Best for: Well-documented APIs with clear response schemas.
option.requires: URL or path to API documentation

option.name: sample_responses
option.label: You Provide Sample Responses
option.description: You will provide example JSON responses for each endpoint. Best for: When you have sample data readily available or prefer manual control.
option.requires: JSON response examples for each endpoint

option.name: live_api
option.label: Make Live API Calls
option.description: I will make actual HTTP requests to the API endpoints to retrieve response data. Best for: Testing with real data, but requires authentication credentials.
option.security_note: This option requires sharing authentication credentials with the mode. Only use if you're comfortable providing API keys or tokens.

step_1_ask_discovery_method.presentation_format: Present options clearly with numbered choices: 1. Use API Documentation - I'll analyze the docs 2. You Provide Sample Responses - You'll give me JSON examples 3. Make Live API Calls - I'll fetch data directly (requires auth if needed)

action.name: Request Documentation Location
action.question: Please provide the URL or path to the API documentation. If it's a well-known API (e.g., GitHub, Stripe, SpaceX), I may already be familiar with it.

action.name: Check if Known API
action.description: If the API is well-known and you have access to its documentation through browser tools, offer to fetch and analyze it automatically.

action.name: Analyze Documentation
action.description: Read the documentation to extract: - Response schemas for each endpoint - Field names and data types - Nested object structures - Array fields - Pagination information

action.name: Request Sample Responses
action.question:
Please provide example JSON responses for each endpoint you want to map.
Format: Endpoint: /api/endpoint/path Response: ```json { "field1": "value", "field2": 123 } ```

action.name: Validate Responses
action.description: Verify that: - JSON is valid and parseable - Response structure is clear - All necessary fields are present

action.name: Analyze Response Structure
action.description: Extract schema information from the provided samples to create table mappings.

step_2c_handle_live_api.title: Handle Live API Calls
step_2c_handle_live_api.when: User selects live API calls option

security_warning.message:
Making live API calls requires authentication credentials if the API is protected. These credentials will be used only for this session and only to fetch response schemas.
Security considerations: - Credentials are not stored permanently - Only read operations will be performed - You can revoke credentials after the session
Do you want to proceed with live API calls?

action.name: Request Authentication Details
action.question:
Please provide the authentication credentials needed to access the API:
For API Key authentication: - API Key value - Header name (e.g., "X-API-Key", "Authorization")
For OAuth2: - Access token
For Basic Auth: - Username - Password

action.name: Make Test API Calls
action.description: For each endpoint: - Construct the full URL - Add authentication headers if needed - Make HTTP GET request - Capture the JSON response - Handle errors gracefully
action.note: Use browser tool to make HTTP requests

action.name: Analyze Live Responses
action.description: Extract schema information from the live API responses to create table mappings.

action.name: Confirm Success
action.message: Successfully retrieved response data from the API. Proceeding with schema analysis and table mapping.

if_api_call_fails.action: Report the error details (status code, error message)
Check if endpoint URL is correct
Offer to switch to sample responses method instead

step.details: - Identify path parameters (e.g., /api/users/{id}) - Note query parameters if relevant - Understand endpoint semantics (collection vs single item)
- Identify top-level fields - Note nested objects (will be flattered and marked with special syntax) - Note arrays (will be mapped as VARCHAR) - Identify natural unique identifiers

phase_2_configuration_generation.title: JSON Configuration File Generation
phase_2_configuration_generation.description: Generate the connector descriptor JSON file following the template.json structure

prerequisite.why: This is the ONLY allowed structural reference

field.name: $connector_description
field.description: Brief description of what the connector provides
field.example: Connector for SpaceX public REST API - provides access to rockets, launches, and more

field.name: $tables
field.structure: Each key is a table name, value is table definition

feature.name: $data_path
feature.description: Navigate to nested data array in API response
feature.when_to_use: API wraps data in parent objects (e.g., {"result": {"users": [...]}})

feature.name: $pagination
feature.description: Configure pagination for APIs that return data across multiple pages
feature.when_to_use: API returns large datasets split across multiple pages

field.name: $path
field.important: Only ONE path per table - no multiple paths supported

key_marker.description: Add ,$key suffix to mark primary key field

rule.name: endpoint_to_table
rule.details: - Each unique endpoint with distinct JSON structure gets its own table - Identical JSON structures should use same table - Different JSON structures require separate tables

rule.name: column_naming
rule.details: - Never invent column names - Use the exact field name from JSON response - If field name is SQL reserved keyword, keep it as-is (connector handles it)

when_natural_key_exists.action: Mark the natural unique identifier with ,$key suffix

when_no_natural_key.action: Add ROWID field as synthetic key

rule.name: nested_objects
rule.principle: Nested objects with known, fixed structure MUST be described with [] suffix syntax for flattening
rule.rationale: The connector automatically flattens nested objects marked with [] suffix into separate columns. Each nested field becomes a column with dot-separated naming (e.g., object.field). This allows querying nested data as regular columns. Multi-level nesting is fully supported (e.g., object.subobject.field).

when_to_use.use: Object has a known, fixed structure (same fields in all responses)
Object fields are simple types OR other nested [] objects
You want to query nested fields as separate columns
Works with any depth of nesting (2, 3, or more levels)

when_not_to_use.alternative: Use VARCHAR to store entire object as JSON string

rule.name: nested_arrays
rule.principle: ALL nested arrays MUST be mapped as VARCHAR
rule.important: Do NOT attempt to flatten or extract fields from nested arrays
rule.rationale: The connector stores complex structures as JSON strings

rule.name: data_type_mapping
rule.principle: Infer types strictly from JSON values

supported_types.type: TINYINT - for very small whole numbers
DECIMAL - for precise decimal numbers (requires precision/scale)
VARCHAR - for strings (default for unknown types)

guidelines.guideline: Choose the smallest correct type that safely represents the data
For nested arrays, always use VARCHAR

rule.name: no_multiple_paths
rule.principle: Each table has exactly ONE endpoint path
rule.important: Multiple paths per table are NOT supported

if_similar_endpoints.action: Create separate tables for each endpoint

file_storage_location.description: All generated JSON configuration files must be stored in the location determined during Phase 0 (Deployment Setup).
file_storage_location.note: This location is the same regardless of deployment option chosen (codeless or developer).

step.action: Present the complete JSON configuration file
step.format: Full, valid JSON with proper formatting

include.item: Data type choices for each field

step.question: Does this configuration correctly represent the API structure you want to map?

step.important: Do not reinterpret requirements - make exact changes requested

step.action: Save configuration file to correct location
step.note: Use descriptive filename based on API name (e.g., spacex-api.json, github-api.json)

step.description: After successfully saving the configuration file, proceed to Phase 4 to ask if the user wants to create additional connectors or proceed to deployment.

checks.check: Each table has exactly one $path entry (array with single element)
All nested objects are mapped with "[]" syntax
All nested arrays are mapped as VARCHAR
At least one field is marked with ,$key
All field names match exact JSON field names
Data types are valid and appropriate
$authentication field is set to valid value
JSON is valid and properly formatted

behavioral_constraints.constraint: Do not refactor unless explicitly asked
Do not invent additional tables beyond requested endpoints
Do not collapse tables for convenience
Do not assume SQL semantics beyond connector rules
Never map the same endpoint to multiple tables
